mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and compress corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns in > 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which directly suppresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.05 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and deceleration in retail sales indicates demand destruction driven by price shocks, which forces equity repricing and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL rises and RSAFS falls, with a combined Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding its 12-month moving average during post-shock recovery phases signals rapid capacity utilization and earnings momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO outperformance fails to generate positive 5-day returns in > 55% of recovery phase observations, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts surpassing 1.6 million units annually triggers a multiplier effect across construction and durable goods sectors, boosting aggregate demand and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in > 60% of periods where HOUST exceeds 1600.0, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll employment surge exceeding 300,000 positions confirms robust labor demand and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases by > 300.0 in a single month
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive skew when PAYEMS exceeds the 300.0 threshold, with a mean return below zero across all historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price levels combined with rising federal funds rates force central banks to prioritize inflation control over growth, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields positive 20-day returns in > 58% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses explanatory power in multivariate regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production expansion outpacing retail sales growth indicates inventory rebuilding and supply chain normalization, which improves corporate margins and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain negative when INDPRO growth exceeds RSAFS growth, or the hypothesis fails to achieve a Rank IC above 0.03 in out-of-sample testing.
